member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AESubscriptionParameters.Active
member.summary: Denotes active or inactive subscription.

remarks.para: If the client deactivates the subscription, then the server will no longer send event notifications to the client based on that subscription, and has no responsibility to buffer or maintain the event notifications. Thus event notifications may be lost.

Even if the subscription is inactive, the Refresh method will still function. In effect, this allows a client to obtain current condition states from time to time (by invoking Refresh) without the need to process event notifications in "real time".

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AESubscriptionParameters.NotificationRate
member.summary: Notification rate of the event subscription.

remarks.para: The notification rate is in milliseconds and tells the server how often to send event notifications. This is a minimum time - do not send event notifications any faster that this UNLESS the buffer size is reached. A value of 0 for notification rate means that the server should send event notifications as soon as it gets them. This property is used to improve communications efficiency between client and server. The notification rate is a recommendation from the client, and the server is allowed to ignore the parameter.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AESubscriptionParameters.Filter
member.summary: Filtering criteria for the event subscription.

remarks.para: Contains object.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AESubscriptionParameters.ReturnedAttributesByCategory
member.summary: Attributes to be returned with event notifications for each event category.

remarks.para: A dictionary where they key is the category ID, and the value is an array of attribute IDs for that event category.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.SubconditionElements
member.summary: Subconditions defined for this condition.

remarks.para: For multi-state conditions, there will be more than one subcondition. For single-state conditions, there will be one subcondition.
This is a keyed collection where the key is the subcondition name, and the value is an .

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.AttributeValues
member.summary: Attributes associated with latest event notification for this condition.

remarks.para: Attributes are vendor-specific.
If a server cannot provide reasonable data for an attribute, the returned VARIANT should is set to VT_EMPTY.
This is a dictionary where the key is attribute ID, and the value is the actual value of that event attribute.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.ActiveSubcondition
member.summary: Currently active sub-condition, for multi-state conditions which are active. For a single-state condition, this contains the information about the condition itself.

remarks.para: Contains an .
For inactive conditions, this value is a NULL pointer.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.Enabled
member.summary: The condition is enabled.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.Active
member.summary: The condition is active.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.Acknowledged
member.summary: The condition is acknowledged.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.Quality
member.summary: Quality associated with the condition state.

remarks.para: Values are as defined for the OPC Quality Flags in the OPC Data Access Server specification.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.ActiveTime
member.summary: Time of the most recent transition into the condition. There may be transitions among the sub-conditions which are more recent.

remarks.para: Contains 0 if the condition has never been active.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.SubconditionActiveTime
member.summary: Time of the most recent transition into active subcondition. This is the time value which must be specified when acknowledging the condition.

remarks.para: Contains 0 if the condition has never been active.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.InactiveTime
member.summary: Time of the most recent transition out of this condition.

remarks.para: Contains 0 if the condition has never been active, or if it is currently active for the first time and has never been exited.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.AcknowledgeTime
member.summary: The time of the most recent acknowledgment of this condition (of any sub-condition).

remarks.para: Contains 0 if the condition has never been acknowledged.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.AcknowledgerId
member.summary: This is the ID of the client who last acknowledged this condition.

remarks.para: Contains an empty string if the condition has never been acknowledged.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionState.Comment
member.summary: The comment string passed in by the client who last acknowledged this condition.

remarks.para: Contains an empty string if the condition has never been acknowledged.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.Message
member.summary: Event notification message describing the event.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.EventType
member.summary: Simple, Condition-Related, or Tracking event.

remarks.para: One of constants.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.CategoryId
member.summary: Event category ID.

remarks.para: Standard and Vendor-specific event category codes.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.Severity
member.summary: Event severity (1..1000).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.AttributeValues
member.summary: Vendor-specific event attributes.

remarks.para: Attributes are vendor-specific.
If a server cannot provide reasonable data for an attribute, the returned VARIANT should be set to VT_EMPTY.
This is a dictionary where the key is attribute ID, and the value is the actual value of that event attribute.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.ConditionName
member.summary: The name of the condition related to this event notification.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.SubconditionName
member.summary: The name of the current sub-condition, for multi-state conditions. For a single-state condition, this contains the condition name.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.Enabled
member.summary: New state of the condition: The condition is enabled.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.Active
member.summary: New state of the condition: The condition is active.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.Acknowledged
member.summary: New state of the condition: The condition is acknowledged.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.Quality
member.summary: Quality associated with the condition.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: Values are as defined for the OPC Quality Flags in the OPC Data Access Server specification.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.AcknowledgeRequired
member.summary: This flag indicates that the related condition requires acknowledgment of this event.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The determination of those events which require acknowledgment is server specific. For example, transition into a LimitAlarm condition would likely require an acknowledgment, while the event notification of the resulting acknowledgment would likely not require an acknowledgment.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.ActiveTime
member.summary: Time that the condition became active (for single-state conditions), or the time of the transition into the current sub-condition (for multi-state conditions).

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: This time is used by the client when acknowledging the condition.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.Cookie
member.summary: Server defined cookie associated with the event notification.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The cookie is used by the client when acknowledging the condition. This value is opaque to the client.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEvent.ActorId
member.summary: Actor ID.

para.b: This property is used only for Tracking Events and for Condition-Related Events which are acknowledgment notifications (see EventType).

remarks.para: For tracking events, this is the actor ID for the event notification.
For condition-related events, this is the AcknowledgerID when the new state is acknowledged. If the AcknowledgerID is an empty string, the event was automatically acknowledged by the server.
For other events, the value is an empty string.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.NodeDescriptor.MakeArgumentString(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Infrastructure.
member.param: Indicates whether an incomplete argument string with just the most important information may be returned.
member.returns: An argument string that represents the node descriptor.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.NodeDescriptor.Match(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.NodeDescriptor)
member.summary: Determines whether the node descriptor matches another node descriptor.
member.param: The node descriptor to match against.
member.returns: Returns true if two node descriptors match; otherwise, returns false.
member.remarks: Two node descriptors match if any information that is present in both node descriptors is equal. For example, if a node Id in one of the node descriptors is not present (null), a node Id in the other node descriptor can be anything, or also not present. But if a node Id is present in both node descriptors, it must be the same node Id. Same rules apply to the browse path portion of the node descriptor.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AECategoryElement.CategoryId
member.summary: Category ID.

remarks.para: Event categories are vendor-specific. Their category IDs can be used in the event subscription for specifying filters. Category IDs are unique across the server (e.g. if Category ID x is used for both simple events and condition events, then the ID references the exact same category.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AECategoryElement.Description
member.summary: Text name or description for the event category.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AECategoryElement.ConditionElements
member.summary: Event conditions supported by this event category.

remarks.para: This is a keyed collection where the key is condition name, and the value is an .

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AECategoryElement.AttributeElements
member.summary: Event attributes that the server can provide as part of an event notification within this event category.

remarks.para: Simple servers may not support any vendor-specific attributes for some or even all event categories.
This is a keyed collection where the key is attribute ID, and the value is an .

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionElement.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string with the condition name.
member.returns: Returns the object contents expressed as readable string.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionElement.Name
member.summary: Condition name. This is the string to be used for display purposes.

remarks.para: Condition names are server specific.
The number of condition names returned will vary depending on the sophistication of the server, but is expected to be less than 30 for most servers.

is in fact allowed to change the available selection at any time. Therefore, a Client should do (or at least allowas an option) a fresh Query every time a selection is to be presented to the end user.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEConditionElement.SubconditionNames
member.summary: An array of subcondition names.

remarks.para: Subcondition names are server specific.
The number of sub-condition names returned will vary depending on the sophistication of the server, but is expected to be less than 10 for most servers.

It is expected that the available subcondition names for a particular condition on the Server will be fairly 'stable' and that they will generally not change 'online'. However, the Server is in fact allowed to change the available selection at any time. Therefore, a Client should do (or at least allow as an option) a fresh Query every time a selection is to be presented to the end user.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AESubconditionElement.Name
member.summary: The name assigned to the sub-condition, e.g. "HighAlarm" for a sub-condition of "LevelAlarm".
member.remarks: In the case of a single-state alarm, the sub-condition name is the same as the associated condition name. The name of the sub-condition is unique within its associated condition.

member.summary: The severity of any event notifications generated on behalf of this sub-condition.
member.remarks: Remark: different sub-conditions of the same condition may have different severity levels.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.Callback
member.summary: A callback method to be invoked for each OPC event.
member.value: If this is a null reference, only the notification events will be generated, but no callback method will be invoked.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.ServerDescriptor
member.summary: Gets the descriptor of the OPC server belonging to the event subscription.

remarks.para: Contains a object.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.State
member.summary: Gets the state object passed by the caller to the EasyAEClient.SubscribeEvents method.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.Exception
member.summary: Gets the current exception. Contains null reference when no exception.

remarks.para: Contains an Exception object (implements IException), or a null reference.
If an exception has occurred, the Refresh, RefreshComplete, and all XXXXChanged properties (such as EnabledChanged) /// will be False.
This property is not serialized into XML. Instead, is serialized. Deserializing from XML creates a generic object with just the message.

member.summary: Gets the current exception. Contains null reference when no exception.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.ErrorMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets a message that describes the current exception.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.ErrorCode
member.summary: Gets the HRESULT of the error.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.Handle
member.summary: Gets the subscription handle returned by the EasyAEClient.SubscribeEvents method.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.SubscriptionParameters
member.summary: Gets the OPC event subscription parameters associated with the notification.

remarks.para: Contains an AESubscriptionParameters object.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.Refresh
member.summary: Denotes whether this notification was triggered by subscription refresh.

remarks.para: When Refresh is True, the value of True in RefreshComplete tells that the refresh has completed.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.RefreshComplete
member.summary: When True, this notification denotes that the subscription refresh is complete.

remarks.para: RefreshComplete can only be True when Refresh is also True.
When RefreshComplete is True, the Event property contains a null reference.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.EnabledChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that the Enabled state of the condition has changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
It is server-specific whether this property will be True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.ActiveChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that the Active state of the condition has changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
This property will be always True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.AcknowledgedChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that the Acknowledged state of the condition has changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
It is server-specific whether this property will be True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.QualityChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that the Quality of the condition has changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
It is server-specific whether this property will be True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.SeverityChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that the Severity of the condition has changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
It is server-specific whether this property will be True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.SubconditionChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that the subcondition (SubconditionName) of the condition has changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
It is server-specific whether this property will be True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.MessageChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that the Message of the condition has changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
It is server-specific whether this property will be True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.AttributeChanged
member.summary: Indicates to the application that one or more attributes of the condition have changed.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: If the event notification is the result of a Refresh, the value of this property should be ignored.
It is server-specific whether this property will be True for a "new event". (A "new event" is any event resulting from the related condition leaving the Inactive and Acknowledged state.)

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.Event
member.summary: Event notification information.

remarks.para: The Event property contains an object for regular event notifications, and for notifications triggered by subscription refresh (except for RefreshComplete notification).

The Event property contains a null reference when an error occurred (indicating connection loss; an Exception property is filled in this case), when a connection is re-gained (an Exception property contains a null reference in this case), or when the notification indicates that subscription refresh is complete (RefreshComplete property contains True in this case).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAENotificationEventArgs.ChangeMask
member.summary: Indicates to the client which properties of the condition have changed, to have caused the server to send the event notification.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAAccessRights
member.summary: Indicates if the item is read only, write only or read/write.

remarks.para: This is NOT related to security but rather to the nature of the underlying hardware.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAAccessRights.NoAccess
member.summary: The client cannot read or change the data item's value.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAAccessRights.Readable
member.summary: The client can read the data item's value.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAAccessRights.Writable
member.summary: The client can change the data item's value.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAAccessRights.Both
member.summary: The client can read and change the data item's value.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQualityChoice
member.summary: The QualityChoice bit field, i.e. the most significant part of the OPC quality flags.

remarks.para: The OPC specification uses the name "Quality" for this bit field, which makes it possible to confuse with the full OPC quality value. We have made up the name "QualityChoice" in order to reduce the chances for confusion.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQualityChoice.Bad
member.summary: Bad. Value is not useful for reasons indicated by the Substatus.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQualityChoice.Uncertain
member.summary: Uncertain. The quality of the value is uncertain for reasons indicated by the Substatus.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQualityChoice.Good
member.summary: Good. The Quality of the value is Good.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice
member.summary: Status is a combination of QualityChoice and Substatus. Which Substatus values can be used depend on QualityChoice.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.BadNonspecific
member.summary: Non-specific. Substatus for BAD Quality: The value is bad but no specific reason is known.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.UncertainNonspecific
member.summary: Non-specific. Substatus for UNCERTAIN Quality: There is no specific reason why the value is uncertain.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.GoodNonspecific
member.summary: Non-specific. Substatus for GOOD Quality: The value is good. There are no special conditions.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.ConfigError
member.summary: Configuration Error. Substatus for BAD Quality: There is some server specific problem with the configuration. For example the item in question has been deleted from the configuration.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.NotConnected
member.summary: Not Connected. Substatus for BAD Quality: The input is required to be logically connected to something but is not. This quality may reflect that no value is available at this time, for reasons like the value may have not been provided by the data source.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.DeviceFailure
member.summary: Device Failure. Substatus for BAD Quality: A device failure has been detected.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.SensorFailure
member.summary: Sensor Failure. Substatus for BAD Quality: A sensor failure had been detected (the ’Limits’ field can provide additional diagnostic information in some situations).

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.LastKnown
member.summary: Last Known Value. Substatus for BAD Quality: Communications have failed. However, the last known value is available. Remark: The ‘age’ of the value may be determined from the TIMESTAMP in the OPCITEMSTATE.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.CommFailure
member.summary: Comm Failure. Substatus for BAD Quality: Communications have failed. There is no last known value is available.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.OutOfService
member.summary: Out of Service. Substatus for BAD Quality: The block is off scan or otherwise locked. This quality is also used when the active state of the item or the group containing the item is InActive.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.WaitingForInitialData
member.summary: Waiting for Initial Data. Substatus for BAD Quality: After Items are added to a group, it may take some time for the server to actually obtain values for these items. In such cases the client might perform a read (from cache), or establish a ConnectionPoint based subscription and/or execute a Refresh on such a subscription before the values are available. This substatus is only available from OPC DA 3.0 or newer servers.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.LastUsable
member.summary: Last Usable Value. Substatus for UNCERTAIN Quality: Whatever was writing this value has stopped doing so. The returned value should be regarded as ‘stale’. Remark: This differs from a BAD value with Substatus 5 (Last Known Value). That status is associated specifically with a detectable communications error on a ‘fetched’ value. This error is associated with the failure of some external source to ‘put’ something into the value within an acceptable period of time. Remark: The ‘age’ of the value can be determined from the TIMESTAMP in OPCITEMSTATE.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.SensorCal
member.summary: Sensor Not Accurate. Substatus for UNCERTAIN Quality: Either the value has ‘pegged’ at one of the sensor limits (in which case the limit field should be set to 1 or 2) or the sensor is otherwise known to be out of calibration via some form of internal diagnostics (in which case the limit field should be 0).

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.EguExceeded
member.summary: Engineering Units Exceeded. Substatus for UNCERTAIN Quality: The returned value is outside the limits defined for this parameter. Remark: In this case (per the Fieldbus Specification) the ‘Limits’ field indicates which limit has been exceeded but does NOT necessarily imply that the value cannot move farther out of range.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.Subnormal
member.summary: Sub-Normal. Substatus for UNCERTAIN Quality: The value is derived from multiple sources and has less than the required number of Good sources.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAStatusChoice.LocalOverride
member.summary: Local Override. Substatus for GOOD Quality: The value has been Overridden. Typically this is means the input has been disconnected and a manually entered value has been ‘forced’.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DALimitChoice
member.summary: The Limit bit field of OPC quality.

remarks.para: The Limit Field is valid regardless of the QualityChoice and Substatus. In some cases such as Sensor Failure it can provide useful diagnostic information.
Server which do not support Limit return 0 (LimitOk).

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DALimitChoice.LimitOk
member.summary: Not Limited. The value is free to move up or down.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DALimitChoice.LimitLow
member.summary: Low Limited. The value has ‘pegged’ at some lower limit.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DALimitChoice.LimitHigh
member.summary: High Limited. The value has ‘pegged’ at some high limit.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DALimitChoice.LimitConst
member.summary: Constant. The value is a constant and cannot move.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality
member.summary: Represent the quality state for a item's data value.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.BadNonspecific
member.summary: Non-specific. The value is bad but no specific reason is known.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.UncertainNonspecific
member.summary: Non-specific. There is no specific reason why the value is uncertain.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.GoodNonspecific
member.summary: Non-specific. The value is good. There are no special conditions.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.ConfigError
member.summary: Configuration Error. There is some server specific problem with the configuration. For example the item in question has been deleted from the configuration.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.NotConnected
member.summary: Not Connected. The input is required to be logically connected to something but is not. This quality may reflect that no value is available at this time, for reasons like the value may have not been provided by the data source.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.DeviceFailure
member.summary: Device Failure. A device failure has been detected.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.SensorFailure
member.summary: Sensor Failure. A sensor failure had been detected (the ’Limits’ field can provide additional diagnostic information in some situations).

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.LastKnown
member.summary: Last Known Value. Communications have failed. However, the last known value is available. Remark: The ‘age’ of the value may be determined from the TIMESTAMP in the OPCITEMSTATE.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.CommFailure
member.summary: Comm Failure. Communications have failed. There is no last known value is available.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.OutOfService
member.summary: Out of Service. The block is off scan or otherwise locked. This quality is also used when the active state of the item or the group containing the item is InActive.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.WaitingForInitialData
member.summary: Waiting for Initial Data. After Items are added to a group, it may take some time for the server to actually obtain values for these items. In such cases the client might perform a read (from cache), or establish a ConnectionPoint based subscription and/or execute a Refresh on such a subscription before the values are available. This substatus is only available from OPC DA 3.0 or newer servers.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.LastUsable
member.summary: Last Usable Value. Whatever was writing this value has stopped doing so. The returned value should be regarded as ‘stale’. Remark: This differs from a BAD value with Substatus 5 (Last Known Value). That status is associated specifically with a detectable communications error on a ‘fetched’ value. This error is associated with the failure of some external source to ‘put’ something into the value within an acceptable period of time. Remark: The ‘age’ of the value can be determined from the TIMESTAMP in OPCITEMSTATE.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.SensorCal
member.summary: Sensor Not Accurate. Either the value has ‘pegged’ at one of the sensor limits (in which case the limit field should be set to 1 or 2) or the sensor is otherwise known to be out of calibration via some form of internal diagnostics (in which case the limit field should be 0).

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.EguExceeded
member.summary: Engineering Units Exceeded. The returned value is outside the limits defined for this parameter. Remark: In this case (per the Fieldbus Specification) the ‘Limits’ field indicates which limit has been exceeded but does NOT necessarily imply that the value cannot move farther out of range.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.Subnormal
member.summary: Sub-Normal. The value is derived from multiple sources and has less than the required number of Good sources.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.LocalOverride
member.summary: Local Override. The value has been Overridden. Typically this is means the input has been disconnected and a manually entered value has been ‘forced’.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAQuality.QualityChoiceBitField
member.summary: Gets the QualityChoice bit field.

remarks.para: You cannot set the QualityChoice using a property; use SetQualityAndSubstatus method instead. This approach is needed as the semantics of the Status depends on the QualityChoice, and therefore setting just QualityChoice could lead to inconsistent or undesired contents of the DAQuality.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.Root
member.summary: The node element for the root of the address space.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.Name
member.summary: Short user friendly portion of the namespace pointing to the element. This is the string to be used for display purposes.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.BrowsePath
member.summary: Specifies the browse path for the OPC item.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.ItemId
member.summary: The unique identifier for this item.
member.remarks: If the is True and ItemID is an empty string, then this element is a “hint” versus being a valid item. The property contains the hint text. If the value is a null reference, then the server has failed provide the item ID.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.IsBranch
member.summary: The node is a branch (a "folder" the can contain other nodes).

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.IsHint
member.summary: This element is a “hint” leaf versus being a valid item. The property contains the hint text, and ItemID is an empty string.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.IsItem
member.summary: This element is an item, i.e. it is a leaf but not a hint.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.IsLeaf
member.summary: The element is an item that can be used to read, write, and subscribe.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.IsRoot
member.summary: Determines whether this element describes a root of the address space.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElement.HasChildren
member.summary: The element has children and can be used for a subsequent browse.
member.remarks: In some cases this property may contain True even if there are no actual nodes below it, e.g. if it is too consuming for the OPC server to determine if an element has children.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeElementCollection
member.summary: A collection of node elements (), keyed by their names.
member.remarks: This collection is returned by the browsing methods.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.VarType
member.summary: Data type specifications used in OPC "Classic". Contains .NET equivalents of COM VARTYPE.

remarks.para: Certain bitwise operations, such as ORing the VT_ARRAY with the element type, are allowed. Windows has some limitations as to which combinations can be made. Also, different types are permissible in different contexts.
The enumeration contains similar members, but is not well suited for uses other than described there. Do not confuse the two.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor.MachineName
member.summary: Name of the machine. Determines the computer on which the OPC server is located. May be an empty string, in which case the OPC server is regarded to exist on the local computer or at the computer specified for it by DCOM configuration.
member.remarks: Machine resources are named using the naming scheme of the network transport. By default, all UNC ("\\server" or "server") and DNS names ("domain.com", "example.microsoft.com", or "135.5.33.19") names are allowed.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerElement.Clsid
member.summary: Gets or sets OPC server's class identifier (CLSID).
member.remarks: A CLSID is a globally unique identifier that identifies an OPC server. The OPC server needs to register its CLSID with the system.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerElement.ProgId
member.summary: Gets or sets a string value indicating the programmatic identifier (ProgID) of the OPC server.
member.remarks: ProgIDs present a human-readable version of the class identifier (CLSID) used to identify COM/ActiveX objects.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerElement.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets a user readable name for the OPC server.
member.remarks: The description may be presented to the user as the name of an available OPC server.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerElement.Vendor
member.summary: Gets or sets a vendor name.
member.remarks: Vendor name may be empty when unknown, e.g. if obtained through OPCEnum object which does not provide it. It is simply a means of identifying the vendor who supplied the particular OPC server.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerElement.Categories
member.summary: Gets or sets information about which specifications the OPC server claims to support.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerElement.VersionIndependentProgId
member.summary: Gets or sets version-independent programmatic identifier of the OPC server application.

remarks.para: VersionIndependentProgId may be empty when unknown, e.g. if obtained through IOPCServerList interface of OPCEnum object which does not provide it, or when registry does not contain that information. In some cases (e.g. when IOPCServerList2 is used, and the VersionIndependentProgID is not available in the registry), the ProgID will be copied to this property.
The VersionIndependentProgID associates a ProgID with a CLSID.

The version-independent ProgID refers to the OPC server's class and does not change from version to version, instead remaining constant across all versions. It refers to the currently installed version of the OPC server's class. The version-independent ProgID corresponds to the name of the latest version of the OPC server application.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerElement.ServerClass
member.summary: Returns a string usable for identification of the OPC server when passed to other methods. If the is not empty, it is used. Otherwise, if the is not empty, it is used. If both are empty, the string form of server's is returned.
member.remarks: For consistency, the corresponding property or parameter on other objects and methods is also called ServerClass.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.OpcException
member.summary: Exceptions arising from OPC operations.
member.remarks: In general, you cannot safely prevent these exceptions from happening. Various conditions external to your code can cause OPC failures, e.g. network problems, improper OPC server configuration, etc. For this reason, you should always expect that an OPC operation can fail.
